dataset: NCH Copper Point of Sale v3.06 with product catalog derived from real retail product data

pi_items.source: Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 4: SKU CLO-0004, Quantity=39) against physical_count.csv (line 3: SKU CLO-0004, Physical Count=35). Variance = 35 - 39 = -4. Matches original products.csv (SKU CLO-0004, 'Floral White Top', Quantity=39).

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 8: SKU CLO-0011, Quantity=42) against physical_count.csv (line 6: SKU CLO-0011, Physical Count=38). Variance = 38 - 42 = -4. Matches original products.csv (SKU CLO-0011, 'Zipped Jacket', Quantity=42).

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 15: SKU ELE-0090, Quantity=22) against physical_count.csv (line 9: SKU ELE-0090, Physical Count=19). Variance = 19 - 22 = -3. Logitech MX Master 3S is a real product with MSRP $99.99 (verified via logitech.com). Matches original products.csv.

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 16: SKU ELE-0092, Quantity=18) against physical_count.csv (line 10: SKU ELE-0092, Physical Count=14). Variance = 14 - 18 = -4. SanDisk Ultra 128GB USB 3.0 is a real product (SDCZ48-128G). Matches original products.csv.

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 17: SKU ELE-0094, Quantity=13) against physical_count.csv (line 11: SKU ELE-0094, Physical Count=15). Variance = 15 - 13 = +2 (overstock — found in back storage). This is a POSITIVE variance, meaning more physical units than system records. Amazon Echo Dot 5th Gen is a real product with MSRP $49.99.

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 20: SKU JEW-0043, Quantity=15) against physical_count.csv (line 13: SKU JEW-0043, Physical Count=12). Variance = 12 - 15 = -3. Matches original products.csv.

Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 27: SKU HOM-0028, Quantity=29) against physical_count.csv (line 15: SKU HOM-0028, Physical Count=33). Variance = 33 - 29 = +4 (overstock — found in warehouse). This is a POSITIVE variance. Matches original products.csv.

pi_items.source: Cross-referenced store_inventory.csv (line 29: SKU GRO-0063, Quantity=58) against physical_count.csv (line 16: SKU GRO-0063, Physical Count=51). Variance = 51 - 58 = -7. Matches original products.csv.

Programmatically compared all 15 physical_count.csv entries against store_inventory.csv quantities. The 7 items where system_qty == physical_count are: CLO-0002 (20=20), CLO-0008 (40=40), CLO-0010 (31=31), CLO-0017 (29=29), ELE-0088 (37=37), ELE-0097 (35=35), JEW-0053 (10=10). Sorted lists match exactly.

Counted all physical_count.csv items where system quantity (from store_inventory.csv) differs from physical count. Found exactly 8 discrepancies: CLO-0004, CLO-0011, ELE-0090, ELE-0092, ELE-0094, JEW-0043, HOM-0028, GRO-0063.

Computed sum of absolute values of NEGATIVE variances only: |−4| + |−4| + |−3| + |−4| + |−3| + |−7| = 4+4+3+4+3+7 = 25. The two positive variances (ELE-0094: +2, HOM-0028: +4) are correctly excluded from the shrinkage count.

This is a task-defined parameter (synthetic). 15% restocking fee is a common retail practice for electronics returns. Consistent with the existing process_restocking_fee_refund task in this environment which also uses 15%.

Verified against store_inventory.csv (line 12: 'Apple AirPods Pro (2nd Gen)', Price=249.99), original products.csv (SKU ELE-0086, Price=249.99), and real-world pricing (Apple MSRP is $249.00; $249.99 is a common retail price point within $1 of MSRP, verified via Amazon/Best Buy listings).

Computed: $249.99 * (1 - 0.15) = $249.99 * 0.85 = $212.4915, which rounds to $212.49. Verified via Python: round(249.99 * 0.85, 2) == 212.49. This matches the pattern in the existing process_restocking_fee_refund task ($579.99 * 0.85 = $492.99).
Task-defined parameter (synthetic). 30% discount is a standard clearance markdown percentage. Consistent with task description which says '30% off their listed prices'.
Verified against store_inventory.csv (line 8: 'Zipped Jacket', SKU CLO-0011, Price=65.00) and original products.csv (SKU CLO-0011, Price=65.00).
Verified against store_inventory.csv (line 7: 'Black Leather Bag', SKU CLO-0010, Price=30.00) and original products.csv (SKU CLO-0010, Price=30.00).
Computed: $45.50 + $21.00 = $66.50 (pre-tax total for 1x Zipped Jacket at 30% off + 1x Black Leather Bag at 30% off). Verified via Python: 45.50 + 21.00 == 66.50.

pi_items.metadata_value: Copper POS supports: inventory import (CSV), stock adjustment, refunds, price overrides, customer management, inventory export, all referenced in task
pi_items.verified_value: NCH Copper Point of Sale is a real retail POS software by NCH Software (nchsoftware.com). Features confirmed: inventory control, returns/refunds, coupons/discounts, receipt customization, multiple payment forms, tax exemption. CSV import via built-in wizard. Stock adjustment via Items list. Refunds via Tools > Refunds menu.
pi_items.source: Verified via nchsoftware.com/point-of-sale/ and Capterra/SoftwareAdvice reviews. All features referenced in the task (import, stock adjustment, refunds, price overrides, customer creation, export) are documented capabilities of the software. Confirmed during live environment testing that View > Items (Ctrl+T), View > Customers (Ctrl+C), and Tools > Refunds menu items are accessible.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires performing a quarterly inventory reconciliation and financial close in NCH Copper Point of Sale. The agent must import 30 items from store_inventory.csv (10 Clothing, 8 Electronics, 5 Jewelry, 4 Home & Garden, 3 Grocery), then reconcile against physical_count.csv (15 items). Exactly 8 items have discrepancies that need stock adjustment: Floral White Top (39→35, variance -4), Zipped Jacket (42→38, variance -4), Logitech MX Master 3S (22→19, variance -3), SanDisk Ultra 128GB USB (18→14, variance -4), Amazon Echo Dot 5th Gen (13→15, variance +2, this is an OVERSTOCK not shrinkage), Bangle Bracelet (15→12, variance -3), Brown Throw Pillows (29→33, variance +4, also OVERSTOCK), and Pure Coconut Water With Orange (58→51, variance -7). The 7 matching items (no adjustment needed) are: Classic Varsity Top (20), Navy Sports Jacket (40), Black Leather Bag (31), Blue Silk Tuxedo (29), Anker PowerCore 10000 (37), TP-Link Deco M5 Mesh WiFi (35), Gold Bird Necklace (10). Total items adjusted should be 8. Total units of shrinkage (sum of negative variances only) is 25 (4+4+3+4+3+7=25; the two positive variances +2 and +4 are excluded). The agent must also add customer Sheryl Baxter and sell her 1x Apple AirPods Pro (2nd Gen) at $249.99 cash, then process a return with 15% restocking fee — the correct refund amount is $212.49 ($249.99 × 0.85 = $212.4915 ≈ $212.49). The agent must process a clearance sale of 1x Zipped Jacket at $45.50 (30% off $65.00) and 1x Black Leather Bag at $21.00 (30% off $30.00) for a pre-tax total of $66.50. The final quarterly_close.txt report should contain a shrinkage table with pipe-delimited lines for each adjusted item showing Item Name | System Qty | Physical Qty | Variance, plus: 'Total items adjusted: 8', 'Total units of shrinkage: 25', refund amount $212.49, and clearance sale total $66.50. The updated inventory should be exported to final_inventory.csv on the Desktop. Key software navigation: inventory import via CSV import wizard, stock adjustment via Items list (View > Items or Ctrl+T), customer creation via View > Customers (Ctrl+C), refunds via Tools > Refunds, price overrides during sale transaction.
